title: Deployment Considerations for Performance and Scalability
breadcrumbs: Deployment Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Deployment Considerations for Performance and Scalability There are a number of measures you can take to improve performance an scalability when configuring and deploying your application. During deployment, you can do the following to improve performance: Consider placing frequently used Uniface objects, such as UAR files, forms, and services, on the client for faster performance. Balance loads over infrastructure, for example, direct heavy processing loads to high-performance servers, or dynamically direct requests to available servers. Balance loads over time, deferring batch processes to off-peak hours. Tune the database to use database-specific functionality. If the application has been designed or implemented to be DBMS-independent, it is possible to switch to a more powerful DBMS. However, this reduces portability and makes it more difficult to change the database. Install additional computing resources, such as additional memory, higher-speed servers, or additional data storage. For more information about improving performance with deployed applications, see Uniface Memory Use , Load Balancing , Uniface Servers , and Database Tuning . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
